title: Characters
summary: Current cast notes, first-impression roles, and relationship hooks.
intro: These are less like final biographies and more like first impressions: who each person feels like on screen, what space they occupy in the group, and where their story could bend.
facts: Ali and Amy are the clearest visual anchors in the current material.
These notes are meant to catch tone and chemistry before every canon detail is fixed.
As the cast grows, each of these could split into a dedicated entry.

sections.heading: Ali
paragraphs: Ali reads like the grounded presence in the group: practical, watchful, and easy to imagine as the first person players learn to trust.
There is a steadiness to the silhouette and gear that makes Ali feel less theatrical than dependable, which is useful when the rest of the world starts getting strange.
items: Visual hook: practical gear and a grounded silhouette.
Narrative role: likely guide, scout, or first companion.
Emotional read: steady enough to anchor riskier personalities.

sections.heading: Amy
paragraphs: Amy brings a different energy. Where Ali feels grounded, Amy feels sharper and more interpretive, like someone who notices the second layer of a situation before anyone else does.
That contrast makes the pair useful early on because their differences can show up in both dialogue and decision-making without much exposition.
items: Visual hook: a portrait that reads differently from Ali even at thumbnail size.
Narrative role: strategist, support, or emotional counterweight.
Dialogue texture: quicker, sharper, and a little harder to read.

sections.heading: Relationship Notes
paragraphs: The relationship layer matters because the cast should not feel like isolated profile cards. The more the camp scenes and field choices affect how people read each other, the more these notes become worth keeping.
items: Track the major trust shifts after each turning point.
Mark which camp upgrades unlock new conversations.
Keep spoilers tucked deeper in the entry once the final order is set.
